Feeling uncertain she climbed into bed, eyes heavy, and fell asleep to the rhythmic reminder that hallucinations don't smell like pine and ash. *** “You look like a goddess,” Lexa said. They’d exchanged numbers and had been talking ever since. He was a year older than them, and was a computer engineer. When Lexa talked about him, they were complete opposites, but somehow, it was working. Lexa blushed. “I really like him.” Persephone grinned. “You deserve it, Lex.” “Thank you.” Lexa popped back into her room to finish getting ready and Persephone went to look for her clutch when her doorbell rang. “I’ll get it!” she called to Lexa.
